qa_pairs.question: How can one view the extended attributes assigned to each archived file when using `dar`?
qa_pairs.answer: To see which extended attributes are assigned to each archived file, use the `dar -l all -alist-ea` command.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if you try to extract files with `lustre.lov` extended attributes to node-local storage?
qa_pairs.answer: If one tries to extract files with the `lustre.lov` extended attribute to node local storage (also known as `$SLURM_TMPDIR`), the extraction will show error messages like `Error while adding EA lustre.lov : Operation not supported`.

qa_pairs.question: Which flag can be used to prevent error messages when extracting Lustre-formatted files to a non-Lustre location?
qa_pairs.answer: To avoid error messages when extracting files to a location not formatted in Lustre, the `-u` flag can be used to exclude a specific type of attribute, for example: `dar -R restore/ -O -w -x all -v -g test -u 'lustre*'`.

qa_pairs.question: How can `lustre.lov` extended attributes be excluded while creating a `dar` archive?
qa_pairs.answer: To get rid of the `lustre.lov` attribute while creating the archive, use the `-u` flag, for example: `dar -w -c all -g test -u 'lustre*'`.

qa_pairs.question: When is it necessary to exclude `lustre.lov` attributes when working with `dar` archives?
qa_pairs.answer: Excluding `lustre.lov` attributes is necessary only if you intend to extract files to a location not formatted in Lustre.

qa_pairs.question: How do you create differential and incremental backups with `dar`?
qa_pairs.answer: You can create differential and incremental backups with `dar` by passing the base name of the reference archive with the `-A` flag.

qa_pairs.question: Give an example of creating a full backup named `monday` for a directory `test`.
qa_pairs.answer: To create a full backup, you would use: `dar -w -c monday -g test`.

qa_pairs.question: How would you create an incremental backup named `tuesday` based on a previous backup named `monday` for the `test` directory?
qa_pairs.answer: To create an incremental backup named `tuesday` using `monday` as a reference, you would use: `dar -w -A monday -c tuesday -g test`.

qa_pairs.question: In an incremental backup scenario, if `monday.1.dar`, `tuesday.1.dar`, and `wednesday.1.dar` exist, what files does `wednesday.1.dar` contain?
qa_pairs.answer: The file `wednesday.1.dar` contains only the files that were modified on Wednesday, but not the files from Monday or Tuesday.

qa_pairs.question: If you have a full backup from Monday and incremental backups from Tuesday and Wednesday, how do you restore all files using `dar`?
qa_pairs.answer: To restore everything, you have to go through all backups in chronological order: `dar -R restore -O -w -x monday`, then `dar -R restore -O -w -x tuesday`, and finally `dar -R restore -O -w -x wednesday`.

qa_pairs.question: What `dar` flag is used to limit the maximum size of each slice in bytes?
qa_pairs.answer: To limit the maximum size of each slice in bytes, use the flag `-s` followed by a number and one of k/M/G/T (e.g., `100M`).

qa_pairs.question: If an archive is split into multiple slices, for instance, `monday.{1..14}.dar`, how do you extract from all of them?
qa_pairs.answer: To extract from all slices, use their base name, for example: `dar -O -x monday`.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find bash functions to help with `dar` usage?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find bash functions written by a team member as inspiration for their own scripts on GitHub, at [https://github.com/razoumov/sharedSnippets here].
